defaultMevTaxThreshold"}},"MaxMevSwapFeePercentageSet(uint256)":{"params":{"maxMevSwapFeePercentage":"The new value for maxMevSwapFeePercentage."}},"MevTaxEnabledSet(bool)":{"params":{"enabled":"The new value for mevTaxEnabled. If true, MEV tax will be charged"}},"MevTaxExemptSenderAdded(address)":{"params":{"sender":"The address of the sender registered as MEV tax-exempt"}},"MevTaxExemptSenderRemoved(address)":{"params":{"sender":"The address of the sender removed from the MEV tax-exempt list"}},"PoolMevTaxMultiplierSet(address,uint256)":{"params":{"newPoolMevTaxMultiplier":"The new value for the pool multiplier","pool":"The address of the pool where the multiplier has changed"}},"PoolMevTaxThresholdSet(address,uint256)":{"params":{"newPoolMevTaxThreshold":"The new value for the pool threshold","pool":"The address of the pool where the threshold has changed"}}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"addMevTaxExemptSenders(address[])":{"params":{"senders":"Addresses of senders to be registered as MEV tax-exempt"}},"getActionId(bytes4)":{"params":{"selector":"The 4-byte selector of the permissioned function"},"returns":{"_0":"The computed actionId"}},"getDefaultMevTaxMultiplier()":{"details":"The MEV swap fee percentage is calculated as `mevTaxMultiplier * priorityGasPrice`, where priorityGasPrice is defined as `transactionGasPrice - baseFee`. This leads to a trade-off that requires careful calibration of the mevTaxMultiplier to incentivize both searchers and LPs. A higher mevTaxMultiplier will raise the swap fee for searchers and accrue more priority fees for LPs. However, raising the mevTaxMultiplier too high may raise searchers' priority fees to levels more typical of retail users, making it difficult for the contract to distinguish between them.","returns":{"_0":"The default MEV Tax Multiplier"}},"getDefaultMevTaxThreshold()":{"details":"The MEV swap fee percentage is only applied if the priority gas price, defined as `transactionGasPrice - baseFee`, is greater than the threshold.","returns":{"_0":"The default MEV Tax Threshold"}},"getHookFlags()":{"details":"The Vault will only call hooks the pool says it supports, and of course only if a hooks contract is defined (i.e., the `poolHooksContract` in `PoolRegistrationParams` is non-zero). `onRegister` is the only \"mandatory\" hook.","returns":{"_0":"Flags indicating which hooks the contract supports"}},"getMaxMevSwapFeePercentage()":{"details":"The absolute minimum is still the static swap fee percentage of the pool. In other words: - if `maxMevSwapFeePercentage > staticSwapFeePercentage`, then `staticSwapFeePercentage <= computedFeePercentage <= maxMevSwapFeePercentage` - if `maxMevSwapFeePercentage <= staticSwapFeePercentage, then `computedFeePercentage = maxMevSwapFeePercentage`"},"getPoolMevTaxMultiplier(address)":{"details":"When a pool is registered with the MEV Hook in the vault, the MEV Hook initializes the multiplier of the pool to the defaultMevTaxMultiplier value. If the pool is not registered with the MEV Hook, it reverts with error MevCaptureHookNotRegisteredForPool(pool).","params":{"pool":"Address of the pool with the multiplier"},"returns":{"_0":"The multiplier of the pool"}},"getPoolMevTaxThreshold(address)":{"details":"When a pool is registered with the MEV Hook in the vault, the MEV Hook initializes the multiplier of the pool with the defaultMevTaxMultiplier value. If the pool is not registered with the MEV Hook, it reverts with error MevCaptureHookNotRegisteredForPool(pool).","params":{"pool":"Address of the pool with the multiplier"},"returns":{"_0":"The threshold of the pool"}},"isMevTaxEnabled()":{"details":"If MEV Tax is disabled, all swaps will pay the static swap fee amount.","returns":{"_0":"True if the MEV Tax is enabled"}},"isMevTaxExemptSender(address)":{"details":"A MEV tax-exempt sender pays only the static swap fee percentage, regardless of the priority fee.","params":{"sender":"The sender being checked for MEV tax-exempt status"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the sender is MEV tax-exempt"}},"onAfterAddLiquidity(address,address,uint8,uint256[],uint256[],uint256,uint256[],bytes)":{"details":"Called if the `shouldCallAfterAddLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. The Vault will ignore `hookAdjustedAmountsInRaw` unless `enableHookAdjustedAmounts` is true. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.","params":{"amountsInRaw":"Actual amounts of tokens added, sorted in token registration order","amountsInScaled18":"Actual amounts of tokens added, sorted in token registration order","balancesScaled18":"Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order","bptAmountOut":"Amount of pool tokens minted","kind":"The add liquidity operation type (e.g., proportional, custom)","pool":"Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)","router":"The address (usually a router contract) that initiated an add liquidity operation on the Vault","userData":"Additional (optional) data provided by the user"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement","_1":"New amountsInRaw, potentially modified by the hook"}},"onAfterInitialize(uint256[],uint256,bytes)":{"details":"Called if the `shouldCallAfterInitialize` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.","params":{"bptAmountOut":"Amount of pool tokens minted during initialization","exactAmountsIn":"Exact amounts of input tokens","userData":"Optional, arbitrary data sent with the encoded request"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the pool accepts the initialization results"}},"onAfterRemoveLiquidity(address,address,uint8,uint256,uint256[],uint256[],uint256[],bytes)":{"details":"Called if the `shouldCallAfterRemoveLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. The Vault will ignore `hookAdjustedAmountsOutRaw` unless `enableHookAdjustedAmounts` is true. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.","params":{"amountsOutRaw":"Actual amount of tokens to receive, sorted in token registration order","amountsOutScaled18":"Scaled amount of tokens to receive, sorted in token registration order","balancesScaled18":"Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order","bptAmountIn":"Amount of pool tokens to burn","kind":"The type of remove liquidity operation (e.g., proportional, custom)","pool":"Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)","router":"The address (usually a router contract) that initiated a remove liquidity operation on the Vault","userData":"Additional (optional) data provided by the user"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement","_1":"New amountsOutRaw, potentially modified by the hook"}},"onAfterSwap((uint8,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,address,address,bytes))":{"details":"Called if the `shouldCallAfterSwap` flag is set in the configuration. The Vault will ignore `hookAdjustedAmountCalculatedRaw` unless `enableHookAdjustedAmounts` is true. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.","params":{"params":"Swap parameters (see above for struct definition)"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement","_1":"New amount calculated, potentially modified by the hook"}},"onBeforeAddLiquidity(address,address,uint8,uint256[],uint256,uint256[],bytes)":{"details":"Called if the `shouldCallBeforeAddLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.","params":{"balancesScaled18":"Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order","kind":"The add liquidity operation type (e.g., proportional, custom)","maxAmountsInScaled18":"Maximum amounts of input tokens","minBptAmountOut":"Minimum amount of output pool tokens","pool":"Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)","router":"The address (usually a router contract) that initiated an add liquidity operation on the Vault","userData":"Optional, arbitrary data sent with the encoded request"},"returns":{"success":"True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement"}},"onBeforeInitialize(uint256[],bytes)":{"details":"Called if the `shouldCallBeforeInitialize` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.","params":{"exactAmountsIn":"Exact amounts of input tokens","userData":"Optional, arbitrary data sent with the encoded request"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the pool wishes to proceed with initialization"}},"onBeforeRemoveLiquidity(address,address,uint8,uint256,uint256[],uint256[],bytes)":{"details":"Called if the `shouldCallBeforeRemoveLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.","params":{"balancesScaled18":"Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order","kind":"The type of remove liquidity operation (e.g., proportional, custom)","maxBptAmountIn":"Maximum amount of input pool tokens","minAmountsOutScaled18":"Minimum output amounts, sorted in token registration order","pool":"Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)","router":"The address (usually a router contract) that initiated a remove liquidity operation on the Vault","userData":"Optional, arbitrary data sent with the encoded request"},"returns":{"success":"True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement"}},"onBeforeSwap((uint8,uint256,uint256[],uint256,uint256,address,bytes),address)":{"details":"Called if the `shouldCallBeforeSwap` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.","params":{"params":"Swap parameters (see PoolSwapParams for struct definition)","pool":"Pool address, used to get pool information from the Vault (poolData, token config, etc.)"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement"}},"onComputeDynamicSwapFeePercentage((uint8,uint256,uint256[],uint256,uint256,address,bytes),address,uint256)":{"details":"Called if the `shouldCallComputeDynamicSwapFee` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.","params":{"params":"Swap parameters (see PoolSwapParams for struct definition)","pool":"Pool address, used to get pool information from the Vault (poolData, token config, etc.)","staticSwapFeePercentage":"18-decimal FP value of the static swap fee percentage, for reference"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement","_1":"Value of the swap fee percentage, as an 18-decimal FP value"}},"onRegister(address,address,(address,uint8,address,bool)[],(bool,bool,bool,bool))":{"details":"Returns true if registration was successful, and false to revert the pool registration. Make sure this function is properly implemented (e.g. check the factory, and check that the given pool is from the factory). The Vault address will be msg.sender.","params":{"factory":"Address of the pool factory (contract deploying the pool)","liquidityManagement":"Liquidity management flags indicating which functions are enabled","pool":"Address of the pool","tokenConfig":"An array of descriptors for the tokens the pool will manage"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the hook allowed the registration, false otherwise"}},"removeMevTaxExemptSenders(address[])":{"params":{"senders":"Addresses of senders to be removed from the MEV tax-exempt list"}},"setDefaultMevTaxMultiplier(uint256)":{"details":"The multiplier is not validated or limited by any value and can assume any 18-decimal number. That's because the multiplier value depends on the priority gas price used by searchers in a given moment for a specific chain. However, the resulting swap fee percentage, given by `priorityGasPrice * multiplier`, is capped at the lower end by the static swap fee, and at the upper end by the maximum swap fee percentage of the vault. Therefore, a multiplier with value 0 will effectively disable the MEV tax, since the static swap fee will be charged. Also, a very high multiplier will make the trader pay the maximum configured swap fee which can be close to 100%, effectively disabling swaps.","params":{"newDefaultMevTaxMultiplier":"18-decimal used to calculate the MEV swap fee percentage"}},"setDefaultMevTaxThreshold(uint256)":{"details":"The threshold can be any unsigned integer and represents the priority gas price, in wei. It's used to check whether the priority gas price level corresponds to a retail or searcher swap. The threshold value is not capped by any value, since it depends on the chain state. A very high threshold (above the priority gas price of searchers in the chain) will disable the MEV tax and charge the static swap fee.","params":{"newDefaultMevTaxThreshold":"The new default threshold"}},"setMaxMevSwapFeePercentage(uint256)":{"details":"See `getMaxMevSwapFeePercentage` for reference; this maximum applies only when `maxMevSwapFeePercentage > staticSwapFeePercentage`. Capped by MAX_FEE_PERCENTAGE defined by the Vault."},"setPoolMevTaxMultiplier(address,uint256)":{"details":"The multiplier is not validated or limited by any value and can assume any 18-decimal number. That's because the multiplier value depends on the priority gas price used by searchers in a given moment for a specific chain. If the pool is not registered with the MEV Hook, it reverts with error MevCaptureHookNotRegisteredForPool(pool). However, the resulting swap fee percentage, given by `priorityGasPrice * multiplier`, is capped in the lower end by the static swap fee, and at the upper end by the maximum swap fee percentage of the vault. Therefore, a multiplier with value 0 will effectively disable the MEV tax, since the static swap fee will be charged. Also, a very high multiplier will make the trader pay the maximum configured swap fee which can be close to 100%, effectively disabling swaps.","params":{"newPoolMevTaxMultiplier":"New multiplier to be set in a pool","pool":"Address of the pool with the multiplier"}},"setPoolMevTaxThreshold(address,uint256)":{"details":"The threshold can be any unsigned integer and represents the priority gas price, in wei. It's used to check whether the priority gas price level corresponds to a retail or searcher swap. The threshold value is not capped by any value, since it depends on the chain state. If the pool is not registered with the MEV Hook, it reverts with error MevCaptureHookNotRegisteredForPool(pool). A very high threshold (above the priority gas price of searchers in the chain) will disable the MEV tax and charge the static swap fee.","params":{"newPoolMevTaxThreshold":"The new threshold to be set in a pool","pool":"Address of the pool with the threshold"}}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"errors":{"InvalidBalancerContractRegistry()":[{"notice":"The `BalancerContractRegistry` set in the constructor is invalid."}],"MevCaptureHookNotRegisteredInPool(address)":[{"notice":"The pool was not registered with the MEV Hook contract."}],"MevSwapFeePercentageAboveMax(uint256,uint256)":[{"notice":"The new max MEV swap fee percentage is above the allowed absolute maximum."}],"MevTaxExemptSenderAlreadyAdded(address)":[{"notice":"The sender is already registered as MEV FB6YS1x8T1Aq9FoDC7LKC4BA"]}},"version":1}

params.senders: Addresses of senders to be registered as MEV tax-exempt

params.selector: The 4-byte selector of the permissioned function

getDefaultMevTaxMultiplier().details: The MEV swap fee percentage is calculated as `mevTaxMultiplier * priorityGasPrice`, where priorityGasPrice is defined as `transactionGasPrice - baseFee`. This leads to a trade-off that requires careful calibration of the mevTaxMultiplier to incentivize both searchers and LPs. A higher mevTaxMultiplier will raise the swap fee for searchers and accrue more priority fees for LPs. However, raising the mevTaxMultiplier too high may raise searchers' priority fees to levels more typical of retail users, making it difficult for the contract to distinguish between them.

getDefaultMevTaxThreshold().details: The MEV swap fee percentage is only applied if the priority gas price, defined as `transactionGasPrice - baseFee`, is greater than the threshold.

getHookFlags().details: The Vault will only call hooks the pool says it supports, and of course only if a hooks contract is defined (i.e., the `poolHooksContract` in `PoolRegistrationParams` is non-zero). `onRegister` is the only "mandatory" hook.

returns._0: Flags indicating which hooks the contract supports

getMaxMevSwapFeePercentage().details: The absolute minimum is still the static swap fee percentage of the pool. In other words: - if `maxMevSwapFeePercentage > staticSwapFeePercentage`, then `staticSwapFeePercentage <= computedFeePercentage <= maxMevSwapFeePercentage` - if `maxMevSwapFeePercentage <= staticSwapFeePercentage, then `computedFeePercentage = maxMevSwapFeePercentage`

getPoolMevTaxMultiplier(address).details: When a pool is registered with the MEV Hook in the vault, the MEV Hook initializes the multiplier of the pool to the defaultMevTaxMultiplier value. If the pool is not registered with the MEV Hook, it reverts with error MevCaptureHookNotRegisteredForPool(pool).

params.pool: Address of the pool with the multiplier

getPoolMevTaxThreshold(address).details: When a pool is registered with the MEV Hook in the vault, the MEV Hook initializes the multiplier of the pool with the defaultMevTaxMultiplier value. If the pool is not registered with the MEV Hook, it reverts with error MevCaptureHookNotRegisteredForPool(pool).

params.pool: Address of the pool with the multiplier

isMevTaxEnabled().details: If MEV Tax is disabled, all swaps will pay the static swap fee amount.

returns._0: True if the MEV Tax is enabled

isMevTaxExemptSender(address).details: A MEV tax-exempt sender pays only the static swap fee percentage, regardless of the priority fee.

params.sender: The sender being checked for MEV tax-exempt status

returns._0: True if the sender is MEV tax-exempt

onAfterAddLiquidity(address,address,uint.details: Called if the `shouldCallAfterAddLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. The Vault will ignore `hookAdjustedAmountsInRaw` unless `enableHookAdjustedAmounts` is true. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.amountsInRaw: Actual amounts of tokens added, sorted in token registration order
params.amountsInScaled18: Actual amounts of tokens added, sorted in token registration order
params.balancesScaled18: Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order
params.kind: The add liquidity operation type (e.g., proportional, custom)
params.pool: Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)
params.router: The address (usually a router contract) that initiated an add liquidity operation on the Vault
params.userData: Additional (optional) data provided by the user

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement
returns._1: New amountsInRaw, potentially modified by the hook

onAfterInitialize(uint256[],uint256,byte.details: Called if the `shouldCallAfterInitialize` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.bptAmountOut: Amount of pool tokens minted during initialization
params.userData: Optional, arbitrary data sent with the encoded request

returns._0: True if the pool accepts the initialization results

onAfterRemoveLiquidity(address,address,u.details: Called if the `shouldCallAfterRemoveLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. The Vault will ignore `hookAdjustedAmountsOutRaw` unless `enableHookAdjustedAmounts` is true. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.amountsOutRaw: Actual amount of tokens to receive, sorted in token registration order
params.amountsOutScaled18: Scaled amount of tokens to receive, sorted in token registration order
params.balancesScaled18: Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order
params.bptAmountIn: Amount of pool tokens to burn
params.kind: The type of remove liquidity operation (e.g., proportional, custom)
params.pool: Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)
params.router: The address (usually a router contract) that initiated a remove liquidity operation on the Vault
params.userData: Additional (optional) data provided by the user

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement
returns._1: New amountsOutRaw, potentially modified by the hook

onAfterSwap((uint8,address,address,uint2.details: Called if the `shouldCallAfterSwap` flag is set in the configuration. The Vault will ignore `hookAdjustedAmountCalculatedRaw` unless `enableHookAdjustedAmounts` is true. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.params: Swap parameters (see above for struct definition)

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement
returns._1: New amount calculated, potentially modified by the hook

onBeforeAddLiquidity(address,address,uin.details: Called if the `shouldCallBeforeAddLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.balancesScaled18: Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order
params.kind: The add liquidity operation type (e.g., proportional, custom)
params.minBptAmountOut: Minimum amount of output pool tokens
params.pool: Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)
params.router: The address (usually a router contract) that initiated an add liquidity operation on the Vault
params.userData: Optional, arbitrary data sent with the encoded request

returns.success: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement

onBeforeInitialize(uint256[],bytes).details: Called if the `shouldCallBeforeInitialize` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.userData: Optional, arbitrary data sent with the encoded request

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with initialization

onBeforeRemoveLiquidity(address,address,.details: Called if the `shouldCallBeforeRemoveLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.balancesScaled18: Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order
params.kind: The type of remove liquidity operation (e.g., proportional, custom)
params.maxBptAmountIn: Maximum amount of input pool tokens
params.minAmountsOutScaled18: Minimum output amounts, sorted in token registration order
params.pool: Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)
params.router: The address (usually a router contract) that initiated a remove liquidity operation on the Vault
params.userData: Optional, arbitrary data sent with the encoded request

returns.success: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement

onBeforeSwap((uint8,uint256,uint256[],ui.details: Called if the `shouldCallBeforeSwap` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.params: Swap parameters (see PoolSwapParams for struct definition)
params.pool: Pool address, used to get pool information from the Vault (poolData, token config, etc.)

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement

onComputeDynamicSwapFeePercentage((uint8.details: Called if the `shouldCallComputeDynamicSwapFee` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.params: Swap parameters (see PoolSwapParams for struct definition)
params.pool: Pool address, used to get pool information from the Vault (poolData, token config, etc.)
params.staticSwapFeePercentage: 18-decimal FP value of the static swap fee percentage, for reference

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement
returns._1: Value of the swap fee percentage, as an 18-decimal FP value

onRegister(address,address,(address,uint.details: Returns true if registration was successful, and false to revert the pool registration. Make sure this function is properly implemented (e.g. check the factory, and check that the given pool is from the factory). The Vault address will be msg.sender.

params.factory: Address of the pool factory (contract deploying the pool)
params.liquidityManagement: Liquidity management flags indicating which functions are enabled
params.tokenConfig: An array of descriptors for the tokens the pool will manage

returns._0: True if the hook allowed the registration, false otherwise

params.senders: Addresses of senders to be removed from the MEV tax-exempt list

setDefaultMevTaxMultiplier(uint256).details: The multiplier is not validated or limited by any value and can assume any 18-decimal number. That's because the multiplier value depends on the priority gas price used by searchers in a given moment for a specific chain. However, the resulting swap fee percentage, given by `priorityGasPrice * multiplier`, is capped at the lower end by the static swap fee, and at the upper end by the maximum swap fee percentage of the vault. Therefore, a multiplier with value 0 will effectively disable the MEV tax, since the static swap fee will be charged. Also, a very high multiplier will make the trader pay the maximum configured swap fee which can be close to 100%, effectively disabling swaps.

params.newDefaultMevTaxMultiplier: 18-decimal used to calculate the MEV swap fee percentage

setDefaultMevTaxThreshold(uint256).details: The threshold can be any unsigned integer and represents the priority gas price, in wei. It's used to check whether the priority gas price level corresponds to a retail or searcher swap. The threshold value is not capped by any value, since it depends on the chain state. A very high threshold (above the priority gas price of searchers in the chain) will disable the MEV tax and charge the static swap fee.

setMaxMevSwapFeePercentage(uint256).details: See `getMaxMevSwapFeePercentage` for reference; this maximum applies only when `maxMevSwapFeePercentage > staticSwapFeePercentage`. Capped by MAX_FEE_PERCENTAGE defined by the Vault.

setPoolMevTaxMultiplier(address,uint256).details: The multiplier is not validated or limited by any value and can assume any 18-decimal number. That's because the multiplier value depends on the priority gas price used by searchers in a given moment for a specific chain. If the pool is not registered with the MEV Hook, it reverts with error MevCaptureHookNotRegisteredForPool(pool). However, the resulting swap fee percentage, given by `priorityGasPrice * multiplier`, is capped in the lower end by the static swap fee, and at the upper end by the maximum swap fee percentage of the vault. Therefore, a multiplier with value 0 will effectively disable the MEV tax, since the static swap fee will be charged. Also, a very high multiplier will make the trader pay the maximum configured swap fee which can be close to 100%, effectively disabling swaps.

params.newPoolMevTaxMultiplier: New multiplier to be set in a pool
params.pool: Address of the pool with the multiplier

setPoolMevTaxThreshold(address,uint256).details: The threshold can be any unsigned integer and represents the priority gas price, in wei. It's used to check whether the priority gas price level corresponds to a retail or searcher swap. The threshold value is not capped by any value, since it depends on the chain state. If the pool is not registered with the MEV Hook, it reverts with error MevCaptureHookNotRegisteredForPool(pool). A very high threshold (above the priority gas price of searchers in the chain) will disable the MEV tax and charge the static swap fee.

params.newPoolMevTaxThreshold: The new threshold to be set in a pool
params.pool: Address of the pool with the threshold

addMevTaxExemptSenders(address[]).notice: Registers a list of senders as MEV tax-exempt senders.

disableMevTax().notice: Permissioned function to reversibly disable charging the MEV Tax in registered pools.

enableMevTax().notice: Permissioned function to enable charging the MEV Tax in registered pools.

getActionId(bytes4).notice: Returns the action identifier associated with the external function described by `selector`.

getDefaultMevTaxMultiplier().notice: Fetch the default multiplier for the priority gas price.

getDefaultMevTaxThreshold().notice: Fetch the default priority gas price threshold.

getHookFlags().notice: Return the set of hooks implemented by the contract.

getMaxMevSwapFeePercentage().notice: Returns the maximum MEV swap fee percentage returned by `onComputeDynamicSwapFeePercentage`.

getPoolMevTaxMultiplier(address).notice: Fetch the priority gas price multiplier of the given pool.

getPoolMevTaxThreshold(address).notice: Fetch the priority gas price threshold of the given pool.

isMevTaxEnabled().notice: Check whether the MEV Tax is enabled in the hook.

isMevTaxExemptSender(address).notice: Checks whether the sender is MEV tax-exempt.

onAfterAddLiquidity(address,address,uint.notice: Hook to be executed after adding liquidity.

onAfterInitialize(uint256[],uint256,byte.notice: Hook to be executed after pool initialization.

onAfterRemoveLiquidity(address,address,u.notice: Hook to be executed after removing liquidity.

onAfterSwap((uint8,address,address,uint2.notice: Called after a swap to perform further actions once the balances have been updated by the swap.

onBeforeAddLiquidity(address,address,uin.notice: Hook to be executed before adding liquidity.

onBeforeRemoveLiquidity(address,address,.notice: Hook to be executed before removing liquidity.

onBeforeSwap((uint8,uint256,uint256[],ui.notice: Called before a swap to give the Pool an opportunity to perform actions.

onComputeDynamicSwapFeePercentage((uint8.notice: Called after `onBeforeSwap` and before the main swap operation, if the pool has dynamic fees.

onRegister(address,address,(address,uint.notice: Hook executed when a pool is registered with a non-zero hooks contract.

removeMevTaxExemptSenders(address[]).notice: Removes a list of senders from the list of MEV tax-exempt senders.

setDefaultMevTaxMultiplier(uint256).notice: Permissioned function to set the default multiplier of the priority gas price.

setDefaultMevTaxThreshold(uint256).notice: Permissioned function to set the default priority gas price threshold.

setMaxMevSwapFeePercentage(uint256).notice: Permissioned function to set the maximum MEV swap fee percentage returned by `onComputeDynamicSwapFeePercentage`.

setPoolMevTaxMultiplier(address,uint256).notice: Permissioned function to set the MEV tax multiplier of a pool, overriding the default value.

setPoolMevTaxThreshold(address,uint256).notice: Permissioned function to set the threshold of a pool, overriding the current value.
